\tzpentry{ID3433}{Translator It Turns Equation}
\paragraph{Canonical Statement.}
ID3433 introduces Translator It Turns Equation as a source-extracted canonical equation candidate. The equation is promoted from the Tier A equation-source intake and remains pending source-context expansion, duplicate review against the earlier TZPID corpus, Wolfram normalization, symbol-key enrichment, and derivation/proof-lane construction.
\paragraph{Canonical Equation.}
$$ This equation is the &#34;translator.&#34; It turns a dimensionless algebraic surplus ($s-1$) into a concrete angular phase error in radians. Let us apply this to the Trawin Constant $\chi = 32/27$ [4]: $$
